ks1.objective: Hit a ball off a tee, then run to a cone and back before the fielders return the ball.
successCriteria: Runs the full course without stopping

lks2.objective: Field a ground ball cleanly and return it to the bowler quickly to limit the batting team's runs.
successCriteria: Gets body behind the ball when fielding
Underarm return to bowler with reasonable accuracy

uks2.objective: Use coordinated team fielding to retrieve and return the ball under pressure, while batters make tactical run choices.
successCriteria: Backs up team-mate without being told
Decides when to attack the ball vs hold position

space.description: Quarter of a sports hall or a 20×15m grass area. Clear run-out zone behind the wicket.

ks1.description: Small area – 15×10m. Keep cones close together so runs are short.

uks2.description: Full half-pitch if outside, 25×15m. Add boundary cones for fielders.

steps: Split into two teams of 4–5: one batting, one fielding.
Bowler bowls underarm to the batter. Batter must run – even if they miss.
Runner goes around the cone circuit and joins the back of their team. 1 point per safe run.
Fielders return the ball to the bowler ASAP – bowler can bowl again immediately.
If batter is caught or bowled (stumps hit), team loses a "bat".
When all 5 bats are lost, teams swap.
questions: How can the fielding team retrieve the ball faster?
Where is the best place to stand if you're not the one fielding the ball?
What's the trade-off between running quickly and being safe?

errors.cause: Doesn't see themselves as a fielder
Technique under pressure / distance too great

step.space: Smaller cone circuit for Y1–2 so runs are quicker
step.task: Fielders cannot move with ball in hand (forces passing)
step.equipment: Tee instead of bowling for Y1–2 / lower ability
step.people: Smaller teams = more turns per child

steps: Two teams of 4–5. One bats, one fields.
Batter hits off a tee – no bowling.
Batter runs to a single cone and back.
Team loses a bat if caught or stumps knocked over.
questions: How did your team work together?
What did you do when the ball came near you?

steps: Two teams of 4–5. Bowler bowls underarm or overarm (coach's call).
Batter must run on every contact. Multiple runs possible if ball is far away.
Fielders return to bowler – bowler must place ball on stumps base, not just hold it.
Lose a bat: caught, bowled, or run out (ball at stumps before runner home).
Award 2 bonus points if fielders make 3+ passes before returning.
